title: Podman v4.9.0: Release notes and final cherry-picks
body:
Not the actual release bump, having issues with my GPG setup.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body: @mheon just checking if bugfixes in release notes have bug ids?
Nope. Most of them are out of the Desktop hack week, and I gather a lot of those are newly discovered

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *mheon*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.9/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5,mheon,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I think farm is documented? I cherry-picked a commit with a lot of manpages in, certainly.
Well, you need to repush. Please `git rm -f docs/source/markdown/podman-farm-build.1.md`.
The bad commit (that git-added podman-farm-build.1.md`) is fee76bda791a10c54cd4935d46a05f4ef5a54745
Repushed. Must have missed whatever commit in main fixed that.

I would encourage you to cherry-pick #21327 as long as you're repushing.

Context: for a long time `podman farm` was a hidden command, so we couldn't do manpage-vs-help checks. And didn't want to anyway because the command was in flux. Now that it's open, let's reenable the check.

Oops, missed my window! It's okay.
Hopefully it passes on main, I'm a little nervous that we haven't been testing this before.
It passes; I checked before submitting. I've been watching the big podman-farm PRs, but must've missed the one where it became unhidden.

/hold
/lgtm
You can't do it this way. You need to either `git-rm` the bad file before it gets added, or in the commit immediately after. Not at the end.
OK, merged it into the docs commit and repushed.
@umohnani8 Can you take a look at the farm errors here? They don't look like flakes.

```
<+201ms> # $ podman farm build --cleanup --local=false --authfile /tmp/podman_bats.O8S6MH/authfile.json --tls-verify=false -t localhost:42469/test-image-2 /tmp/podman_bats.UsVc3f
...
         # Error: build: adding images ["docker://localhost:42469/test-image-2@"] to list: invalid reference format
                                                                      --------^
```
That `@` looks weird

@mheon I am unable to reproduce the error both locally and by spinning up a ci environment. Can we run the test again to see if it happens again? Maybe something else happened with the push (possibly a network error) and we are just seeing an unrelated error message.
Pressed rerun. [link to original failure](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4755280831971328/html/farm-podman-fedora-39-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html)
Test fails consistently, looks like
The man page comments could be chased later.  Please hit the release note if you need to respin, otherwise that too can be done later.  Looks like the farm test is still struggling.
Easily reproduces on a 1mt. @umohnani8 ping me for access. This is not something I can possibly fix.

@mheon this commit is needed also https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21004/commits. We fixed a bug on the push endpoint where the output was never being returned. The failure is happening because we are not getting the manifest digest after the push here.
Once you cherry pick this, the farm tests should pass!
Done, TY @umohnani8
All comments on the release itself addressed. We can hit the manpage fixes on main.

LGTM
fingers crossed tightly for the Farm test happiness

Failure is a known flake. Restarted. Hold still in place so
/lgtm
/hold cancel
